content: Inside every living thing, there are tiny tiny things called cells that are like tiny factories. These cells need instructions to know what to do, like a recipe to make a cake, and that's where something called mRNA comes in. mRNA is like a special messenger that carries the instructions from one part of the cell to another part, telling it what to do. The instructions that mRNA carries are like a secret code, and the cell has a special way of reading that code to know what to do. Think of it like building with Legos, the mRNA is like the instructions that tell you which blocks to use and how to put them together to make a cool castle. When the cell follows the instructions carried by the mRNA, it can make all sorts of important things, like the proteins that make our muscles strong or our skin healthy. So, mRNA is like a tiny little messenger that helps our cells work properly, and without it, our bodies wouldn't be able to function. To sum it up, mRNA is like a special set of instructions that helps our cells make the things they need to keep our bodies healthy and strong.
